reviews.review_title: Brilliant game, maybe not enough content
reviews.review_description: Great game, great concept, but really there just isn't enough of it for me to justify the price. I understand it's early in developement, and for that I want to give the developers a hearty thumbs up as this is one of the best executions of a great concept out there on the store. Unfortunately though it gets quite repetitive quite quickly, and though the extra gamemodes/maps provide some variation, it simply isn't enough for the $39 AUD price. I'll definitely be checking in on this game in the future, but unless you have some good disposable income to invest in an upcoming title, I'd say Davigo (however it's pronounced) is just barely not worth it at this stage.

The core idea here is really fun and I works alright but there are a few issues, especially with the giant’s gameplay that really need some work. Namely, the giant has very limited mobility and very limited ranged attacks which make it very hard to attack the knights due to how mobile they are. Because the knights can pretty much constantly fly or grapple around the giant’s ground pound abilities are next to useless, and since throwing things in vr is a bit of a pain and even if you can you will eventually run out of things to throw, your only good option is the laser blast that you can shoot only after you block missiles. Which has the obvious issue of needing to block a missile in order to use it. Whereas the knights have nearly limitless ammo and limitless mobility.

How do I join a server

developer_response.body:
As the Giant, you can only create a server by selecting ONLINE GAME from the menu. PC players must join your game. If you're having trouble finding PC players, check out our Discord where you can find others wanting to play!

If this was helpful, please consider changing your review. It would help us a lot. Cheers!

I hope the creators read the reviews and I hope they're planning to improve the hell out of it; this could be an amazing game, just needs a lot more work if you're charging 30 freaking dollars!

developer_response.body: VR locomotion has been added in a recent update, which we hope will help players with smaller spaces enjoy the game! The Giant can now grab the environment to pull themselves around the map. Turn on the feature in the Experimental tab in Settings. Let us know what you think and please consider changing your review if you like it. It would help us a lot. Thanks for your feedback!

reviews.review_description:
Davigo is a fun game with great potential.
It can be quite difficult to be the Giant, but you can set custom settings for the Giant or Knight like health, rockets and more. This way you can make each one stronger or weaker. The custom settings can be saved for future use.

Knights are playing free on steam which is essential to have a bigger player base and available knights to play. The inclusion of mod support enhances the game's longevity by introducing a steady stream of new and diverse maps over time.

Please add support for Giant movement as an option, like dragging with the hands. Also maps for 2 Giants could be fun. I'm excited to see how this develops in the future 

If you liked the review, please take a look at my bio for a ref.

The game is very fun but it is extremely hard to get other people to play with. I had to wait for 15 minutes just to get one person in my lobby. This is not the developers fault though. Just a warning that it is hard to actually play with other people.

reviews.review_title: Not much to see, not much to do.
reviews.review_description:
Intro: 

I found out and was excited about Davigo for the better part of the year, As what made me so excited about it in the first place is that it's such a unique idea that not many other games explore. And when I found out that it was in development for a long time I had high hopes that I would receive a finished product... 

Things that you did well:

As mentioned before, the idea is really cool. The concept of two different sides having completely different perspectives fascinates me. Playing as the knight feels fast, zippy, and fun. And the VR controls are very solid besides for the teleporting. I love how not even a week into the games life you're already updating it which does make me feel like they're still open ears listening which is very healthy and a key part in improving your game. 

Things that need more improvement: 

This game lacks incentive, after I play this game for an hour or two and have my fun I don't really feel the need to come back, (i'll give examples on how to fix this later.) But besides for the menu, there's no music and maybe that doesn't seem important to you, but it makes the game feel empty and hollow. I find it baffling that this game can lack such simple features. For example, every time the VR player wants to change a map or a mode they have to go out of their way to exit what they're currently doing back to the lobby to change the map/mode now this may seem like a small thing, but doing this all the time can slow down the pace of the game and make knights leave the lobby because of it. Another simple feature that this game is missing is that this game doesn't even have a randomizer for the maps and modes which I feel like wouldn't take that long to implement, but would help with the game so much to keep it's variety. Now this may come off as harsh, but when you're charging full price for what feels like an unfinished game, the idea alone won't cut it. The price point I find to be a little much for what is essentially 4 mini games once you have your fun, you're left with a $25 hole in your pocket which is truly a shame because I think an idea like this could be really fleshed out. One last thing would be the menu, make it feel more full and alive, give players something to do while waiting in the lobby, if you want examples look at Splatoon 3. 

How to make the game worth its price point:

Keep the price point but add polish, incentive, music, variety to the gameplay, and ALOT of quality of life updates. It does excite me how much potential this game has though and even though I don't plan on playing this game any further, I won't refund it and I'll look out for updates that interest me. 

Suggestions:

all right, so let's start off with incentives, it would be so cool if you could customize your knight/giant. Now how you earn these outfits? It could be through in game currency by playing the game, special events, and by supporting your Patreon. 
Now what I'm about to say, synergizes with variety of gameplay territory, but these cosmetics could give your Knight/giant unique attributes and moves so every lobby you have to strategize and change up your place style, depending on what they are using, similar to dead by daylight. A huge one that may not take as much time would be a ranked mode, obviously have a casual mode too, but make sure give rewards in both modes, but in ranked mode, give bonuses for certain milestones of a certain rank. This would synergies very well with the quality of life balance changes.

Next up is music, i'm not a musician, so I'll leave that up to you, but there should be music, and make it so you can disable it in the settings. 

Next up is variety of gameplay, have variety make people think about what they should do next in any given situation whether that's unique attributes to your player, more power ups/throwable objects, or have the map change throughout the match. The four game modes is a good start, but adding these things can create a huge combination of unique matches. 

Last up is quality of life changes, earlier, which was the constantly having to go back to the lobby, to change a map, and the surprising lack of a random mode and map button. Now, that update you released yesterday was a great start as you made the cannons more fair to fight against, notice how you didn't see me mention those cannons in the bad section haha?
Stuff like this shows to your community that you care and still actively improving the game. which could help people stick around and tell others about your game. 

Conclusion: 

OK let me wrap this up in a big bowtie, Davigo feels hollow and unfinished, at least for now. but I am very excited for you to prove me wrong and to truly see how great of a game this could be. As there are chunks of good in this game but there's not enough meat on the bone to justify $25. I spent 70 minutes on this review, so I hope you take everything I say into consideration, and to not label me as a "hater." I am very excited for what the future holds for DAVIGO!

remember me when you guys get popular hahaha

developer_response.body: Thanks for the thorough review and great suggestions! We really appreciate you took all that time to let us know your thoughts. We agree with a lot of the points you brought up and will continue to work on improving the experience for players like you. Hopefully the updates over the coming weeks and months will address your concerns. Cheers!

reviews.review_title: One of the best VR multi-player games.
reviews.review_description:
Just spent my first 4 hours in this game, both in Knights (PC) and in Giants (VR). This game is a blast, easily one of the best purchases I've made for multi-player games. If anyone is scrolling through the reviews debating on dropping the $26, I'll be writing my full review below.

PROS:
-----------‐---
•Game is the perfect party game. It can support multiple Knights (PC) players per lobby. It's a blast swatting your friend around.

•graphics are clean and fit the style. Simplistic art style that doesn't look cheap and runs well on quest 2.

•modding support. The game supports community made mods, which already features dozens of amazing maps.

•Game is completely free for Knights. While VR users must pay $26 dollars to play as the Giant, Knights can simply go to the steam page and play for free with the Knight's pass. Which I highly respect the developers for doing.

CONS:
-----------‐---
•Occasional server issues, the game sometimes struggles to keep a good connection to the server, and rarely boots you out of the lobby.

•Buggy. A good amount of bugs, both game breaking and small, exist in this game, the Giants (VR) hands sometimes get stuck under the maps, leaving them completely vulnerable. After a few matches, the game will sometimes refuse to load props and spawns for the Knight (PC). Plus, some small audio glitches.

-----------‐---

Overall, this is a solid buy for VR users who are looking for a unique multi-player game. 8/10.
Had a lot of fun with friends best multiplayer game.
This game is one of many but its one of the greatest games that I've seen the characters are great and what you can do in the game is perfect
Hard but fun and good game modes
It's so bad I don't know how to get into lobby's because it doesn't tell you how to

I got used to my vr now not bored but just kinda got used to it and I don't really feel any enjoyment playing it but this made my vr experience fun once again as I can play with my brother in the same room and have some fun or with my friends away from me and the Physics just wow like it's so good Physics if you feeling your starting to get bored you should definitely try this game out and also best thing is if you wanting to play with your friends you can just tell them to get it as it's free on pc so that way you can most definitely play most of the time with your friends

i got the game on the day of release and i've been able to play with some knights finally but i barely have anywhere in my house to move around, even with a moderately large play space i can't reach some places of the map so if you guys could make it so you can fly with a joystick that makes you fly in the direction your facing then i would give the game 5 stars and can you make it so there is a regular speed but on custom game mode you can change the speed you move at? if i can't move around on the  maps the game is nearly unplayable

This game is INCREDIBLY fun. Whether youre playing with randoms, or your friends, the competitive nature of the game makes it very enjoyable.

I will say though, launching the game for the first time, i was surprised to find out how physical the game is! I was sweating about 2 rounds in. 

Four players vs giant is VERY hard and disorienting. Its pretty difficult to keep track of 4 players, all while having the automatic cannons firing at you all game.

The one single bug i did notice however, was that (especially on the lava map) your hands tend to get stuck places. Multiple times i had my hands become stuck under the metal grating in certain maps, resulting in me basically having to stop playing and try to free my hands from underneath the map.

All in all, i will say, this game is incredibly well done, and for release day, has a surprisingly LITTLE amount of bugs or undesired features.

Right off release there is about 7-8 maps, which is more than most games can say.

I had a ton of fun playing this. I’ve literally been recommending it to my friends non-stop since i saw a gameplay trailer on reddit.

VERY well done guys!

_____________

Adding from another review, i forgot to mention, while there ARE a lot of maps, theyre also huge. I played in a roughly 7x7’ area, and still punched a wall once.

The game is very fast paced too, so it’s a LOT easier to get WAY out of your guardian zone than you’d think; very easily.

Youd need a decent sized area to play (at least 10x10 feet) comfortably without worrying anout walls and irl objects.
There are no knights. Pls can knights make lobbies that giants can join so it is more efficient? Or advertise the game more on steam

reviews.review_title: Really fun with a few flaws [25 off]
reviews.review_description:
Find a 25 ref er al in my bio.

I've played a bunch of the Patreon beta before this entered Applab and I really enjoy playing this with my gf.

Pros:
- Unique concept
- Fun gameplay on both sides (grappling hook wheeeeee)
- Convincing non-VR people to play with you is easy since Knights Pass exists
- Mod support will keep this fresh for a while

Cons:
- Controller support feels weird
- You really need some space for this one or you will punch walls, even with guardian on
- No button mapping yet
